primary_accounting_issue: Whether foreign exchange risk on intragroup dividends receivable can be designated as a hedged item in a hedge accounting relationship under IFRS 9 in consolidated financial statements

primary_authority.reason: Paragraph 6.3.5 establishes the general requirement that only items with parties external to the reporting entity can be designated as hedged items; paragraph 6.3.6 provides a specific exception for foreign currency risk of intragroup monetary items in consolidated financial statements

supporting_authority.reason: Governs the treatment of intragroup monetary items and exchange differences upon consolidation, clarifying when intragroup receivables create foreign currency exposure that affects consolidated profit or loss

peripheral_authority.reason: Addresses hedge accounting for net investments in foreign operations, which is a distinct hedging context from intragroup monetary items

treatment_families.family: General prohibition on hedging intragroup items
Exception for foreign currency risk on intragroup monetary items

approaches.label: Intragroup item prohibition
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: Under the general rule in IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.5, hedge accounting is not permitted for intragroup items because they are not with external parties, which would apply to intragroup dividend receivables unless a specific exception applies

approaches.label: Intragroup monetary item exception
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.6 permits hedging the foreign currency risk of intragroup monetary items in consolidated financial statements when the intragroup item is transacted between entities with different functional currencies and the foreign currency risk affects consolidated profit or loss, potentially applicable to intragroup dividend receivables denominated in a currency other than the entity's functional currency
